prompt: A secret is hard-coded in source. What is the correct fix?

options.text: Encrypt the credential with a key also stored in the source
Load the secret at runtime from an environment variable or a secrets manager, and keep it out of source control
Base64-encode the credential in the source

explanation: Externalising the secret (env var / secrets manager) keeps it out of the codebase and lets you rotate it — the correct fix for hard-coded credentials (CWE-798). Encoding, commenting, renaming, or encrypting-with-a-bundled-key all still ship the secret to anyone with the code.
source_reference: CWE-798; OWASP A07:2021 (Identification and Authentication Failures)

prompt: A secret is hard-coded in source. What is the correct fix?

options.text: Load the secret at runtime from an environment variable or a secrets manager, and keep it out of source control
Move the credential into a code comment
Encrypt the credential with a key also stored in the source

explanation: Externalising the secret (env var / secrets manager) keeps it out of the codebase and lets you rotate it — the correct fix for hard-coded credentials (CWE-798). Encoding, commenting, renaming, or encrypting-with-a-bundled-key all still ship the secret to anyone with the code.
